title: Notice of Health Canada’s proposal to enable the use of phosphatidylserine (soy) as a supplemental ingredient in foods – Reference Number: NOP/ADP SI-003 - Canada.ca
content:
## Notice of Health Canada’s proposal to enable the use of phosphatidylserine (soy) as a supplemental ingredient in foods – Reference Number: NOP/ADP SI-003
Health Canada’s Food Directorate completed a detailed premarket safety  assessment of phosphatidylserine for use as a supplemental ingredient in  foods, under the Supplemented  Foods regulatory framework . The  results of the premarket assessment support the safety of  phosphatidylserine derived from soy. Consequently, Health  Canada intends to enable the use of phosphatidylserine (soy) by modifying the List of  Permitted Supplemental Ingredients as described in the Notice of Proposal information document referenced  below.
The purpose of this communication is to publicly announce the  Department's intention in this regard and to provide the appropriate contact  information for any inquiries or for those wishing to submit new scientific  information relevant to the safety of phosphatidylserine (soy) when used  as a supplemental ingredient in foods.

## Information Document(s)
Notice of proposal to enable the  use of phosphatidylserine (soy) as a supplemental ingredient in foods –  Reference Number: NOP/ADP SI-003
To obtain an  electronic copy of Health Canada’s summary of the safety assessment for the use  of phosphatidylserine (soy) as a supplemental ingredient, please contact our publications office or send an e-mail to publications-publications@hc-sc.gc.ca with the subject heading "HPFB BNS Phosphatidylserine  (soy) EN – 2022".
Health Canada's Food Directorate  is committed to reviewing new scientific information on the safety of use of  any permitted supplemental ingredient, including phosphatidylserine  (soy). Anyone wishing to submit new  scientific information on the supplemental ingredient use of  phosphatidylserine (soy) or to  submit inquiries may do so in writing, by regular mail or electronically. If  you wish to contact the Food Directorate electronically, please use the words “ Phosphatidylserine  (soy) (NOP SI-003) ” in the subject line of your e-mail. Health Canada is able to consider  information received by November 20 2022 , 75 days from the date of  this posting.
Supplemented Foods: Bureau of Nutritional Sciences, Food Directorate 251 Sir Frederick Banting Driveway Tunney's Pasture, A.L.: 2203E Ottawa, Ontario K1A 0K9 E-mail: supplementedfoods-alimentssupplementes@hc-sc.gc.ca

## Supporting Information
- Guidance Document: Supplemented  Foods Regulations July 2022
